sutta: then another brahmin visited the exalted 1 greeted him exchanged compliments and sat down at 1 side so seated he said master gotama is there 1 thing which when made become made to increase embraces and establishes 2 wheels wheel here and wheel hereafter there is brahman what is it master gotama verily earnestness brahmin is the 1 thing which when made become made to increase embraces and establishes 2 wheels we'll hear and we'll hereafter brahmin as the footmarks of all prowling creatures are admitted in an elephants and the elephant's foot is counted chief so earnestness is the 1 thing which when made become made to increase embraces and establishes 2 wheels we'll here and we'll hereafter as all rafters in a peaked house reach to the p slope to the p unite in the peak and the peak is counter chief so earnestness brahmin is the 1 thing which when made become made to increase and embraces and establishes 2 wheels we'll hear and we'll hereafter as the light of the stars is not worth a 16th part the light of the moon and the light of the moon is counter-chief so earnestness brahmin is the 1 thing which 1 may become made to increase embraces and establishes 2 wheels we'll hear and we'll hereafter as the end of the sutta
commentary: this word earnestness is the bollywood apamada sometimes translated as diligence or sincerity and it means doing what needs to be done in the spiritual path and 1 where 1 has to be careful with half measures that is compromising um that means not being um earnest or sincere enough for example some persons want to practice the holy life and also want to get married and if they do get married and this shows a lack of earnestness and here that person ends up being neither um successful in this spiritual path or neither does he become a good husband or a good father if we are earnest or sincere we obtain happiness in this life and happiness hereafter and we should note that when we practice the spiritual path correctly and we should be happy not sad now there's some people who think that um because they are contemplating dukkha all the time they they shouldn't be um be full of dukkha but in the damage came to visit the buddha and kissed his feet and this is an indian customer which you find also in sri lanka where lay people kiss the monk's feet and the buddha said and then why ask the king why did he pay so much homage to this body and the king started to praise the buddha all the qualities of the buddha and also the monks under the buddha and the king said that whereas in other external ascetics now you could see that they were lean and wretched looking looking very miserable and unhappy whereas the monks under the buddha they looked very happy and exceedingly happy and joyful and so [Music] we should know that if we practice the holy path and we obtain the bliss of renunciation the happiness of renunciation we should be happy and looking happy and the next utah
